visa_types.name: Volunteer Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
- Passport,       (valid for at least 30 days after the intended stay/departure date from       South Africa; at least Two unused/blank visa pages)
- Fully       completed visa application Form – 8 (DHA-1738) – must be completed in black ink and       block letters.
- Application       Form – 8 (DHA-1738) must be signed by the applicant and signature should       match the signature on the passport.
- Proof       of funds (Last 03 month’s bank statement signed and stamped by the bank       with a balance of South African Currency R 3000.
- Letter       from the applicant detailing purpose and duration
- Letter       from the host organization confirming purpose and duration stay in RSA,       disclosure of salary/stipend, declaration of support On organization       letter head. Identity of the responsible person.
- A medical report completed on doctor’s letter head, Medical Report issued by registered medical practioner on letterhead with regard to applicant's general state of health. Report not older than 3months at time of submission.
- Police clearance certificate from countries where applicants stayed for 12 months or more. Not older than 6months.
- Vaccination certificate, (yellow fever) if applicant will travel through or stay in yellow fever belt. For the latest Yellow Fever Policy please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4LLo2aMcYoC6e6ap7XKTFD/0e3c4baea7e37e157c690b512d5e58a4/revised-yellow.pdf)
- Copies       of passport bio-data pages (photo page and address/parents page.
- Two       recent (not older than 30 days/1 month) passport size, ( 35mm x 45mm of       the picture) photographs
- Original       covering letter signed by the applicant (should clearly mention who will       bear the expenses of the trip)
- Valid return air ticket
- Accommodation proof
Medical, radiological and  police clearance will not be required if the volunteer service is going to be  less than 3 months.
SOME IMPORTANT POINTS
- Applicant needs to book  the appointment for Harare and Bulawayo through this website only before coming  to VFS, without appointment VFS will not process your application.
- Applicant must       apply in Person.
- Payment mode       Cash Only & Credit Card.
- Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in  mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render  the application incomplete:
- All the  supporting documents should be duly certified by the Police in South African  Commissioner or commissioner of Oaths.
- Please be aware       that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and       duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy.
- Please be aware that  applicants should be aware of the processing time for their chosen category of  visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are  minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- Please be aware       that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and       duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it is       not guaranteed that the embassy will grant your request
- Documents issued in a  Regional language must be officially translated to English.
- All application       forms filled by hand must be original in BLACK INK only. Scanned copies or       signatures of application forms will strictly not be accepted.

visa_types.overview: Overview
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

## ALL APPLICANT MUST APPLY IN PERSON
- Proof of financial – 3  months bank statement, certified by bank and not older than 7 days from the  time of application.
- Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear  in mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render  the application incomplete
- There must be 7 working days before the proposed flight itinerary;
- All supporting documents should be duly certified by the Police in South African Commissioner or Commissioner of Oaths;
- Invitation letter from host in South Africa must be signed;
- For minors, certified copy of birth certificate, certified copies of both biological parents’ Passports (main page) and 
an affidavit signed by both biological parents and certified by a Commissioner of Oaths (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/southafrica/zimbabwe/english/pdf/Parental-Consent-Affidavit.pdf) will be required. Additional documents  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5A5b1SZ2PtivbW2gbTJHhT/e78272c25dafe35173b38f66a80a3c52/child-travel.pdf)will be required in case either of the natural parents is unable to sign.
- No fixed flight  arrangements should be made until the visa has been attained.
- Payment mode Cash Only
- Please be aware that all  decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa  are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it is not guaranteed that the  embassy will grant your request.
- Please note applicants  should be aware of the Processing Time for their chosen category of visa and  apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are minimum as  stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- All application forms filled  by hand must be original in BLACK INK only. Scanned copies or signatures of  application forms will strictly not be accepted.

## Visa Fees
- Visa Fee :- USD 24.00 (All Visa Fees are NON REFUNDABLE and they are only payable in USD)
- There is an additional service charge of $75.00 (inclusive of TAX) per application in addition to the visa fees for all applicants. This fee must be paid only at the Visa Application Centre
- The Special Delivery facility for the return of Passports is available at a fee of $10 (Inclusive of TAX) per application. This is an optional service and can be paid at the Visa Application Centre.
- The SMS facility for updates on your application is available at a fee of $2 (Inclusive of TAX) per application. This is an optional service and can be paid at the Visa Application Centre.
- Payment Options - Cash & Card only.

## Documents Required
- Passport,       (valid for at least 30 days after the intended stay/departure date from       South Africa; at least Two unused/blank visa pages)
- Fully       completed visa application Form – 8 (DHA-1738) – must be completed in black ink and       block letters.
- Application       Form – 8 (DHA-1738) must be signed by the applicant and signature should       match the signature on the passport.
- Proof       of funds (Last 03 month’s bank statement signed and stamped by the bank       with a balance of South African Currency R 3000.
- Letter       from the applicant detailing purpose and duration
- Letter       from the host organization confirming purpose and duration stay in RSA,       disclosure of salary/stipend, declaration of support On organization       letter head. Identity of the responsible person.
- A medical report completed on doctor’s letter head, Medical Report issued by registered medical practioner on letterhead with regard to applicant's general state of health. Report not older than 3months at time of submission.
- Police clearance certificate from countries where applicants stayed for 12 months or more. Not older than 6months.
- Vaccination certificate, (yellow fever) if applicant will travel through or stay in yellow fever belt. For the latest Yellow Fever Policy please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4LLo2aMcYoC6e6ap7XKTFD/0e3c4baea7e37e157c690b512d5e58a4/revised-yellow.pdf)
- Copies       of passport bio-data pages (photo page and address/parents page.
- Two       recent (not older than 30 days/1 month) passport size, ( 35mm x 45mm of       the picture) photographs
- Original       covering letter signed by the applicant (should clearly mention who will       bear the expenses of the trip)
- Valid return air ticket
- Accommodation proof
Medical, radiological and  police clearance will not be required if the volunteer service is going to be  less than 3 months.
SOME IMPORTANT POINTS
- Applicant needs to book  the appointment for Harare and Bulawayo through this website only before coming  to VFS, without appointment VFS will not process your application.
- Applicant must       apply in Person.
- Payment mode       Cash Only & Credit Card.
- Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in  mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render  the application incomplete:
- All the  supporting documents should be duly certified by the Police in South African  Commissioner or commissioner of Oaths.
- Please be aware       that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and       duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy.
- Please be aware that  applicants should be aware of the processing time for their chosen category of  visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are  minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- Please be aware       that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and       duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it is       not guaranteed that the embassy will grant your request
- Documents issued in a  Regional language must be officially translated to English.
- All application       forms filled by hand must be original in BLACK INK only. Scanned copies or       signatures of application forms will strictly not be accepted.

## Photo Specifications
Click Here  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/54rKdHPXQfrGjZZPjbg9Xv/d9f77841ca2d2e1ba45abbd1ed698268/photo-spes.pdf)

## Processing Time
The timelines given by South African Embassy  in Zimbabwe for the processing of each application is minimum 6 to 8 weeks from  the day of submission at Visa Application Centers.
However visa applicants should bear in  mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and  processing time may vary. Embassy Visa section may ask for additional documents  which may require more additional working days for processing.       

## Download Forms
 Please download and complete fully all the application forms below, ensure they are signed and please bring with you when attending for your appointment.
  Form 1738 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5Nd1ogjxBLBIcD4j3iQHyE/97bc5bf00d58a42512e5e8f4adfb9388/dha-1738-form-8.pdf)

visa_types.name: Treaty Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
- Application       for DHA-1738 completed in full with BLACK ink and signed by applicant
- A       valid passport which expires in no less than 30 days after expiry of the       intended date of departure (at least Two unused/blank visa pages)
- Two       passport size coloured photographs (35X45mm)
- Original       covering letter signed by the applicant (should clearly mention the       purpose of visit)
- Proof       of sufficient financial means in the form of recently bank certified statements       for the last three months.
- Vaccination certificate, (yellow fever) if applicant will travel through or stay in yellow fever belt. For the latest Yellow Fever Policy please
click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4LLo2aMcYoC6e6ap7XKTFD/0e3c4baea7e37e157c690b512d5e58a4/revised-yellow.pdf)
- A medical report completed on doctor’s letter head, Medical Report issued by registered medical practioner on letterhead with regard to applicant's general state of health. Report not older than 6 months at time of submission.
- Marriage       certificate or in the case of a foreign spousal relationship, proof of       official recognition thereof issued by the authorities of the country       concerned if available
- The       affidavit where a spousal relationship to a South African citizen or       resident is applicable, as well as documentation proving cohabitation and       the extent to which the related financial responsibilities are shared by       the parties and setting out the particulars of children in the spousal       relationship
- Divorce       decree, where applicable
- Court       order granting full or specific parental responsibilities and rights,       where applicable
- Death       certificate, in respect of late spouse, where applicable
- Legal       separation order, where applicable
- Police       clearance certificates in respect of applicants 18 years and older, in       respect of all countries where person resided one year or longer since       having attained the age of 18.
- A       letter from the relevant organ of state which is party to the treaty       attesting to-
- the       nature of the program me
- participation       of the foreigner in the specified program me
- the       type of activities the foreigner is expected to perform and the duration       thereof
- Accommodation       of the foreigner
- Any       other relevant details pertaining to the foreigner's stay in the South       Africa
- A       written undertaking by the sending or receiving organ of state accepting       responsibility for the costs related to the deportation of the applicant       and his or her dependent family members, should it become necessary.

- A  letter from the foreign state institution or learning institution of the       foreign state confirming the particulars of the applicant, the applicant's       enrolment with a learning institution abroad, and the date on which the       program me
In the case of a program  me of cultural, economic or social exchange, organized or administered by an  organ of state or a learning institution, in conjunction with a learning  institution or a foreign state institution: 
- Proof       of a valid return air ticket or written undertaking by the organ of state,       learning institution or employer accepting responsibility for the return       or deportation costs of the applicant, as the may be
- Proof       of medical cover for the duration of the exchange period with a medical       scheme registered in terms of the Medical Schemes Act, 1998 (Act No. 131       of 1998)
- A       letter from the organ of state or learning institution confirming the       existence of the exchange program me; or A letter from the foreign       institution confirming the enrolment of the applicant or the foreign state       institution conducting the program me, as the case may be

Overview
Exchange Visas may be issued to  foreigners who are not older than 25 years of age and wish to participate in  cultural, economic or social exchange programs administered by an organ of the  State or a public higher educational institution in conjunction with an organ  of a foreign state.

            The validity period of an exchange permit depends on the length of the program  for which it is issued and usually does not exceed 12 months. Exchange permits  cannot be renewed or extended. Should you successfully apply for an exchange  permit, you will not qualify for a temporary residence permit until you have  lived outside the Republic of South Africa for at least 12 months.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Exchange Visas may be issued to  foreigners who are not older than 25 years of age and wish to participate in  cultural, economic or social exchange programs administered by an organ of the  State or a public higher educational institution in conjunction with an organ  of a foreign state.

            The validity period of an exchange permit depends on the length of the program  for which it is issued and usually does not exceed 12 months. Exchange permits  cannot be renewed or extended. Should you successfully apply for an exchange  permit, you will not qualify for a temporary residence permit until you have  lived outside the Republic of South Africa for at least 12 months.

## Visa Fees
- Visa Fee :- USD 24.00 (All Visa Fees are NON REFUNDABLE and they are only payable in USD)
- There is an additional service charge of $75.00 (inclusive of TAX) per application in addition to the visa fees for all applicants. This fee must be paid only at the Visa Application Centre
- The Special Delivery facility for the return of Passports is available at a fee of $10 (Inclusive of TAX) per application. This is an optional service and can be paid at the Visa Application Centre.
- The SMS facility for updates on your application is available at a fee of $2 (Inclusive of TAX) per application. This is an optional service and can be paid at the Visa Application Centre.
- Payment Options - Cash & Card only.

## Documents Required
- Application       for DHA-1738 completed in full with BLACK ink and signed by applicant
- A       valid passport which expires in no less than 30 days after expiry of the       intended date of departure (at least Two unused/blank visa pages)
- Two       passport size coloured photographs (35X45mm)
- Original       covering letter signed by the applicant (should clearly mention the       purpose of visit)
- Proof       of sufficient financial means in the form of recently bank certified statements       for the last three months.
- Vaccination certificate, (yellow fever) if applicant will travel through or stay in yellow fever belt. For the latest Yellow Fever Policy please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4LLo2aMcYoC6e6ap7XKTFD/0e3c4baea7e37e157c690b512d5e58a4/revised-yellow.pdf)
- A       medical report completed on doctor’s letter head, Medical Report issued by       registered medical practioner on letterhead with regard to applicant's       general state of health. Report not older than 6 months at time of       submission.
- A       radiological report completed on doctor’s letter head, Radiological Report       issued by a registered radiologist on letterhead certifying that the       applicant has been examined by him or her with regard to active pulmonary       TB signs. Report not older than 6 months at time of submission. Not       applicable to under 12 years and pregnant women.
- Marriage       certificate or in the case of a foreign spousal relationship, proof of       official recognition thereof issued by the authorities of the country       concerned if available
- The       affidavit where a spousal relationship to a South African citizen or       resident is applicable, as well as documentation proving cohabitation and       the extent to which the related financial responsibilities are shared by       the parties and setting out the particulars of children in the spousal       relationship
- Divorce       decree, where applicable
- Court       order granting full or specific parental responsibilities and rights,       where applicable
- Death       certificate, in respect of late spouse, where applicable
- Legal       separation order, where applicable
- Police       clearance certificates in respect of applicants 18 years and older, in       respect of all countries where person resided one year or longer since       having attained the age of 18.
- Proof       of a valid return air ticket or written undertaking by the organ of state,       learning institution or employer accepting responsibility for the return       or deportation costs of the applicant, as the case may be
- Proof       of medical cover for the duration of the exchange period with a medical       scheme registered in terms of the Medical Schemes Act, 1998 (Act No 131 of       1998)
- A  letter from the Department of Basic Education, or Higher Education and Training, or the learning institution in the Republic confirming that it       is responsible for organizing or administering the existence of the       program me, outlining the activities, terms and conditions and duration       thereof; and accepting full responsibility for the student whilst he or       she is in the South Africa
- A  letter from the foreign state institution or learning institution of the       foreign state confirming the particulars of the applicant, the applicant's       enrolment with a learning institution abroad, and the date on which the       program me
In the case of a program  me of cultural, economic or social exchange, organized or administered by an  organ of state or a learning institution, in conjunction with a learning  institution or a foreign state institution: 
- Proof       of a valid return air ticket or written undertaking by the organ of state,       learning institution or employer accepting responsibility for the return       or deportation costs of the applicant, as the may be
- Proof       of medical cover for the duration of the exchange period with a medical       scheme registered in terms of the Medical Schemes Act, 1998 (Act No. 131       of 1998)
- A       letter from the organ of state or learning institution confirming the       existence of the exchange program me; or A letter from the foreign       institution confirming the enrolment of the applicant or the foreign state       institution conducting the program me, as the case may be
SOME IMPORTANT POINTS
- Applicant needs to book  the appointment for Harare and Bulawayo through this website only before coming  to VFS, without appointment VFS will not process your application.
- Applicant must       apply in Person.
- Payment mode       Cash Only & Credit Card.
- Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in  mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render  the application incomplete:
- All the  supporting documents should be duly certified by the Police in South African  Commissioner or commissioner of Oaths.
- Please be aware that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy.
- Please be aware that  applicants should be aware of the processing time for their chosen category of  visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are  minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- Please be aware       that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it is not guaranteed that the embassy will grant your request
- Documents issued in a  Regional language must be officially translated to English.
- All application forms filled by hand must be original in BLACK INK only. Scanned copies or signatures of application forms will strictly not be accepted.

The timelines given by South African Embassy  in Zimbabwe for the processing of each application is minimum 6 to 8 Weeks from  the day of submission at Visa Application Centers.

## Overview

## ALL APPLICANT MUST APPLY IN PERSON
- Proof of financial – 3  months bank statement, certified by bank and not older than 7 days from the  time of application.
- Incomplete applications will  not be accepted. Please, bear in mind that failing to meet any of the following  requirements will also render the application incomplete
- There must be 7 working days before the proposed flight itinerary;
- All supporting documents should be duly certified by the Police in South African Commissioner or Commissioner of Oaths;
- Invitation letter from host in South Africa must be signed;
- No fixed flight  arrangements should be made until the visa has been attained.
For minors, certified copy of birth certificate, certified  copies of both biological parents’ Passports (main page) and an affidavit signed by both biological parents and certified by  a Commissioner of Oaths (http://www.vfsglobal.com/southafrica/zimbabwe/pdf/parentalconsentaffidavit.pdf) will be  required. Additional documents (http://www.vfsglobal.com/southafrica/zimbabwe/pdf/childtravel.pdf) will be required in case either of the natural parents is unable  to sign.

- Please note applicants  should be aware of the Processing Time for their chosen category of visa and  apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are minimum as  stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- All application forms filled  by hand must be original in BLACK INK only. Scanned copies or signatures of  application forms will strictly not be accepted.
- A transit visa shall-
- In the case of air transit,  be issued for a period not exceeding 24 hours; and
- In the case of land transit,  be issued for a period not exceeding 48 hours.
Transit by air:
Applicants who are transiting by air  from the following airports are exempt from obtaining a transit visa:
- O R Tambo International
- Cape Town International
- King Shaka
- Lanseria
Those transiting by sea or road to  neighbouring countries will still require a transit visa and must lodge their  applications at any of the South African Visa Application Centres by booking an  appointment here (schedule-an-appointment.html).

Overview
If you are a student and wish to study in South Africa you can  apply for a study visa by completing Form BI-1738. 
Visas issued for studies at higher education and training  institution shall be issued for duration of the course, but not exceeding four  years;
Visas issued for studies at school shall be valid for the  duration of the studies; provided that the duration for studies at the primary  school shall not exceed eight years and for secondary school shall not exceed  five years.

## Overview
If you are a student and wish to study in South Africa you can  apply for a study visa by completing Form BI-1738. 
Visas issued for studies at higher education and training  institution shall be issued for duration of the course, but not exceeding four  years;
Visas issued for studies at school shall be valid for the  duration of the studies; provided that the duration for studies at the primary  school shall not exceed eight years and for secondary school shall not exceed  five years.

Duration And Purpose Of Stay
The period of residence in the RSA will be determined from the  applicant’s date of admission to the Republic in accordance with any conditions  that may appear on the visa. Applicants must ensure that they request the full  duration of their intended stay. 
Applicants must ensure that they apply for the correct visa  reflecting the true purpose of the stay, as any change in the purpose of stay will not be approved in the RSA .
Warning
Any applicant allowed entry  into South Africa due to any misrepresentation, or false declaration on the  application form or who sojourns in the Republic in contravention of his/her  visa conditions shall be guilty of an offence and liable on conviction to a  fine or to imprisonment as an illegal foreigner. 
Any applicant who overstays as contemplated in section  50(1) of the Immigration Act shall be informed of his or her undesirability.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Foreigners who are  contemplating investing in the South African economy by establishing a business  or by investing in an existing business in the country must apply for a  business visa, if it is his or her intention to be employed in the business.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Foreigners who are  contemplating investing in the South African economy by establishing a business  or by investing in an existing business in the country must apply for a  business visa, if it is his or her intention to be employed in the business. 

## Visa Fees
- Visa Fee :- USD 84 (All Visa Fees are NON REFUNDABLE)
- There is an additional service charge of $75.00 (inclusive of TAX) per application in addition to the visa fees for all applicants. This fee must be paid only at the Visa Application Centre
- The Special Delivery facility for the return of Passports is available at a fee of $10 (Inclusive of TAX) per application. This is an optional service and can be paid at the Visa Application Centre.
- The SMS facility for updates on your application is available at a fee of $2 (Inclusive of TAX) per application. This is an optional service and can be paid at the Visa Application Centre.
- Payment Options – USD Cash only for visa fees.

## Documents Required
- Application for DHA-1738 completed in full with BLACK ink and signed by applicant. Only handwritten application forms are accepted in Black INK only.
- A       valid passport which expires in no less than 30 days after expiry of the       intended date of departure (at least Two unused/blank visa pages)
- Two       passport size coloured photographs (35X45mm)
- Original       covering letter signed by the applicant (should clearly mention the       purpose of visit)
- Proof       of sufficient financial means in the form of recently bank certified statements       for the last three months.
- Vaccination certificate, (yellow fever) if applicant will travel through or stay in yellow fever belt. For the latest Yellow Fever Policy please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4LLo2aMcYoC6e6ap7XKTFD/0e3c4baea7e37e157c690b512d5e58a4/revised-yellow.pdf)
- A medical report completed on doctor’s letter head, Medical Report issued by registered medical practioner on letterhead with regard to applicant's general state of health. Report not older than 3 months at time of submission. All Medical and radiological reports must be supported with separate letter from doctor confirming that the reports have been issued based on personal examination by said doctor.
- Marriage       certificate or in the case of a foreign spousal relationship, proof of       official recognition thereof issued by the authorities of the country       concerned if available
- The       affidavit where a spousal relationship to a South African citizen or       resident is applicable, as well as documentation proving cohabitation and       the extent to which the related financial responsibilities are shared by       the parties and setting out the particulars of children in the spousal       relationship
- Divorce       decree, where applicable
- Court       order granting full or specific parental responsibilities and rights,       where applicable
- Death       certificate, in respect of late spouse, where applicable
- Written       consent from both parents and court order granting full parental       responsibilities and rights parent, where applicable
- Proof       of legal adoption where applicable
- Legal       separation order, where applicable
- Police       clearance certificates in respect of applicants 18 years and older, in       respect of all countries where person resided one year or longer since       having attained the age of 18.
- A       certificate issued by a chartered accountant registered with the South       African Institute of Chartered Accountants or a professional accountant       registered with the South African Institute of Professional Accountants to       the effect that:-
- at least an amount in  cash to be invested in the Republic as determined from time to time by the  Minister, after consultation with the Minister of Trade and Industry, by notice  in the Gazette, is available; or
- at least an amount in  cash and a capital contribution as determined from time to time by the  Minister, after consultation with the Minister of Trade and Industry, by notice  in the Gazette is available
- Undertaking by the  applicant that at least 60% of the total staff compliment to be employed in the  operations of the business shall be South African citizens or permanent  residents employed permanently in various positions: Provided that proof of  compliance with this undertaking shall be submitted within 12 months of  issuance of the visa
- An       undertaking to register with the -
- South African Revenue  Service
- Unemployment Insurance  Fund
- Compensation Fund for  Occupational Injuries and Diseases
- Companies and  Intellectual Properties Commission (CPIC); where legally required
- Relevant professional  body, board or council recognized by SAQA in terms of section 13(1)(i) of the  National Qualifications Framework Act where applicable: Provided that upon  registration, all certificates shall be submitted to the Director-General
- A       letter of recommendation from the Department of Trade and Industry       regarding -
- The feasibility of the  business
- The contribution to  the national interest of South Africa
Additional supporting  documents in respect of a business visa: In respect of a business visa by a  foreigner who has established a business or invested in an existing business in  South Africa 
- A  certificate issued by a chartered accountant registered with the South       African Institute of Chartered Accountants or a professional accountant       registered with the South African Institute of Professional Accountants to the effect that:
- at least an amount in  cash to be invested in the Republic as determined from time to time by the  Minister, after consultation with the Minister of Trade and Industry, by notice  in the Gazette, is available or already invested; or;
- at least an amount in  cash and a capital contribution as determined from time to time by the  Minister, after consultation with the Minister of Trade and Industry, by notice  in the Gazette, is available or already invested
- proof that at least  60% of the total staff compliment employed in the operations of the business  are South African citizens or permanent residents employed permanently in  various positions
- Proof       of registration with the -
- South African Revenue  Service
- Unemployment Insurance  Fund
- Compensation Fund for  Occupational Injuries and Diseases
- Companies and  Intellectual Properties Commission (CPIC), where legally required
- Relevant professional  body, board or council recognized by SAQA in terms of section 13(1)(i) of the  National Qualifications Framework Act, where applicable
- A       letter of recommendation from the Department of Trade and Industry       regarding -
- The feasibility of the  business
- The contribution to  the national interest of the Republic
- A       foreigner who invests or has invested in an existing business shall, in       addition, submit -
- Financial statement in  respect of the preceding financial year
- Proof of investment
- The       applicant must, within 12 months of the visa being issued, submit to the       Director-General a letter of confirmation from the Department of Labour,       that 60% of the staff compliment employed in the operations of the       business are South African citizens or permanent residents who are       employed permanently in various positions
SOME IMPORTANT POINTS
- Applicant needs to book  the appointment for Harare and Bulawayo through this website only before coming  to VFS, without appointment VFS will not process your application.
- Applicant must       apply in Person.
- Payment mode USD Cash Only for visa fees.
- Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in  mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render  the application incomplete:
- All the  supporting documents should be duly certified by the Police in South African  Commissioner or commissioner of Oaths.
- Please be aware       that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and       duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy.
- Please be aware that  applicants should be aware of the processing time for their chosen category of  visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are minimum  as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- Please be aware       that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and       duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it is       not guaranteed that the embassy will grant your request
- Documents issued in a  Regional language must be officially translated to English.
- All application       forms filled by hand must be original in BLACK INK only. Scanned copies or       signatures of application forms will strictly not be accepted.

## Photo Specifications
Click Here  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/54rKdHPXQfrGjZZPjbg9Xv/d9f77841ca2d2e1ba45abbd1ed698268/photo-spes.pdf)

## Processing Time
The timelines given by South African Embassy in Zimbabwe for the  processing of each application is minimum 06 to 08 weeks from the day of  submission at Visa Application Centers.
However visa applicants should bear in mind that each  application is subject to an individual assessment and processing time may  vary. Embassy Visa section may ask for additional documents which may require  more additional working days for processing.

visa_types.overview: Overview
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

## ALL APPLICANT MUST APPLY IN PERSON
- Proof of financial – 3  months bank statement, certified by bank and not older than 7 days from the  time of application.
- Incomplete applications will  not be accepted. Please, bear in mind that failing to meet any of the following  requirements will also render the application incomplete
- There must be 7 working days before the proposed  flight itinerary;
- All supporting documents should be duly  certified by the Police in South African  Commissioner or Commissioner of Oaths;
- Invitation letter from host in South Africa must be signed;
- No fixed flight  arrangements should be made until the visa has been attained.
- For minors, certified copy of birth certificate, certified copies of both biological parents’ Passports (main page) and an affidavit signed by both biological parents and certified by  a Commissioner of Oaths (http://www.vfsglobal.com/southafrica/zimbabwe/pdf/parentalconsentaffidavit.pdf)
will be required. Additional documents (http://www.vfsglobal.com/southafrica/zimbabwe/pdf/childtravel.pdf) will be required in case either of the natural parents is unable to sign.
- Payment       mode Cash Only
- Please       be aware that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries       and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it       is not guaranteed that the embassy will grant your request.
- Please       note applicants should be aware of the Processing Time for their       chosen category of visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all       processing times are minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- All       application forms filled by hand must be original in BLACK INK only.       Scanned copies or signatures of application forms will strictly not be       accepted.

## Visa Fees
- Visa Fee :- USD 24.00 (All Visa Fees are NON REFUNDABLE and they are only payable in USD)
- There is an additional service charge of $75.00 (inclusive of TAX) per application in addition to the visa fees for all applicants. This fee must be paid only at the Visa Application Centre
- The Special Delivery facility for the return of Passports is available at a fee of $10 (Inclusive of TAX) per application. This is an optional service and can be paid at the Visa Application Centre.
- The SMS facility for updates on your application is available at a fee of $2 (Inclusive of TAX) per application. This is an optional service and can be paid at the Visa Application Centre.
- Payment Options – USD Cash only for visa fees

## Documents Required

Please print  Checklist here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3GWcoEZ7rpO9P2RijSMREt/7075a830922b8c83c67f74e70a80ba82/requirments-visa-checklist.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
Click Here for Photo Specification (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/54rKdHPXQfrGjZZPjbg9Xv/d9f77841ca2d2e1ba45abbd1ed698268/photo-spes.pdf)

## Processing Time
The timelines given by South African Embassy  in Zimbabwe for the processing of each application is minimum 6 to 8 weeks from the day of submission at Visa Application Centers.
However visa applicants should bear in  mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and  processing time may vary. Embassy Visa section may ask for additional documents  which may require more additional working days for processing.
Withdrawal of the Application.
If the applicant wishes to withdraw his/her application after the submission of the application at VFS Centers in Harare & Bulawayo, below are the requirements.
- A withdrawal form is required to be filled. This can be collected from the VFS Centre
- A covering letter mentioning the reason for the withdrawal.
- A VFS ICR copy, a copy of international passports bio –page

- No       fixed flight arrangements should be made until the visa has been       attained.
- All       supporting documents should be duly  certified by the Police in South       African Commissioner or Commissioner of Oaths;
- Invitation letter from host and RSA id copy/Permit and passport copy (certified). The invitation letter from the host in South Africa must be signed;
- Marriage       certificate, where applicable (For verification purpose applicant needs to       carry the Original Marriage Certificate at the time of submission at VFS       center)
- Copies       of passport bio-data pages
- Original       covering letter signed by the applicant stating the name, passport number       of applicant(s) and duration of visit. Letter must have the physical       address, as well as contact details of authorized signature’s name,       designation and signature (should clearly mention who will bear the       expenses of the trip)
- For       Minor Dependent Children
- Documents such as Parental Consent Affidavit form for which the link is below, passport copy of parents as well as child + Unabridged Birth Certificate of child must be carried for Parental Consent Affidavit procedure. In case of death of either parents death certificate has to be produced. In case of death of both parents the procedure has to be carried out by legal guardian at Mumbai Consulate and death certificates mandatory of both parents to be produced. In case of divorce the legal divorce documents has to be produced.
- Affidavit less than 3 months old as part of the application. Originals should be kept for their records while travelling Minors who apply for a South African visa at VFS service-point shall be required to submit the attested copy of Parental Consent to South Africa.
- If unabridged birth certificate is in local language than English translation affidavit must be produce along with local language unabridged birth certificate.
Some important points
- Applicant needs to  book the appointment for Harare and Bulawayo through this website only before  coming to VFS, without appointment VFS will not process your application.
- Applicant       must apply in Person.
- Payment mode Usd Cash Only for visa fees.

- Please       be aware that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries       and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy.
- Please be aware that  applicants should be aware of the processing time for their chosen category of  visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are  minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- Please       be aware that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries       and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it       is not guaranteed that the embassy will grant your request
- Documents issued in a  Regional language must be officially translated to English.
- All       application forms filled by hand must be original in BLACK INK only.       Scanned copies or signatures of application forms will strictly not be       accepted.
- Frequent       Travelers – Applicant travelling more than once a year may apply for a       Multiple entries for up to 3 Years

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Visitor Visa for Less than 90 days will be issued  for the following purpose. 
- Visits to family or friends and also for tourism purposes
- Business purposes
- Spouse to join a spouse who is in South Africa on a work or study permit
- Children to join parents who are in South Africa on work or study permits
- Fiancée/ fiancé to join his or her partner with the intention of marrying within 90 days
- Study purposes (max stay 3-months)
- Research
- Conferences
- To work in the production of a movie or show (i.e. in the entertainment       industry)

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Visitor Visa for Less than 90 days will be issued  for the following purpose. 
- Visits to family or friends and also for tourism purposes
- Business purposes
- Spouse to join a spouse who is in South Africa on a work or study permit
- Children to join parents who are in South Africa on work or study permits
- Fiancée/ fiancé to join his or her partner with the intention of marrying within 90 days
- Study purposes (max stay 3-months)
- Research
- Conferences
- To work in the production of a movie or show (i.e. in the entertainment       industry)
- For medical purposes (max stay 3-months)
- Sport events

## Visa Fees
- Visa Fee :- USD 24.00 (All Visa Fees are NON REFUNDABLE and they are only payable in USD)
- There is an additional service charge of $75.00 (inclusive of TAX) per application in addition to the visa fees for all applicants. This fee must be paid only at the Visa Application Centre
- The Special Delivery facility for the return of Passports is available at a fee of $10 (Inclusive of TAX) per application. This is an optional service and can be paid at the Visa Application Centre.
- The SMS facility for updates on your application is available at a fee of $2 (Inclusive of TAX) per application. This is an optional service and can be paid at the Visa Application Centre.
- Payment Options – USD Cash only for visa fees .

## Documents Required
- Passport, (valid for at least 30 days after the intended stay/departure date from  South Africa; at least Two unused/blank visa pages)
- Two recent (not older than 30 days/1 month) passport size, ( 35mm x 45mm of  the picture) photographs
- Fully completed visa application form-11 (DHA-84) – must be completed in black ink and block letters.  Only handwritten application forms are accepted in Black INK only.
- Application form-11 (DHA-84) must be signed by the applicant and signature should       match the signature on the passport.
- Vaccination  certificate, (yellow fever) if applicant will travel through or stay in yellow fever belt. For the latest Yellow Fever Policy please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/TWzzxJPgIXtS4UFr8F6d5/0d5795c042ea690061d3981d1baea953/yellow-fever-belt.pdf)
- Proof       of financial – 3 months bank statement, certified by bank and not older       than 7 days from the time of application. Incomplete applications will not       be accepted.
- There       must be 7 working days before the proposed flight itinerary;
- No       fixed flight arrangements should be made until the visa has been       attained.
- All       supporting documents should be duly  certified by the Police in South       African Commissioner or Commissioner of Oaths;
- Invitation letter from host and RSA id copy/Permit and passport copy (certified). The invitation letter from the host in South Africa must be signed;
- Marriage       certificate, where applicable (For verification purpose applicant needs to       carry the Original Marriage Certificate at the time of submission at VFS       center)
- Copies       of passport bio-data pages
- Original       covering letter signed by the applicant stating the name, passport number       of applicant(s) and duration of visit. Letter must have the physical       address, as well as contact details of authorized signature’s name,       designation and signature (should clearly mention who will bear the       expenses of the trip)
- For       Minor Dependent Children
- Documents such as Parental Consent Affidavit form for which the link is below, passport copy of parents as well as child + Unabridged Birth Certificate of child must be carried for Parental Consent Affidavit procedure. In case of death of either parents death certificate has to be produced. In case of death of both parents the procedure has to be carried out by legal guardian at Mumbai Consulate and death certificates mandatory of both parents to be produced. In case of divorce the legal divorce documents has to be produced.
- Affidavit less than 3 months old as part of the application. Originals should be kept for their records while travelling Minors who apply for a South African visa at VFS service-point shall be required to submit the attested copy of Parental Consent to South Africa.
- If unabridged birth certificate is in local language than English translation affidavit must be produce along with local language unabridged birth certificate.
Some important points
- Applicant needs to  book the appointment for Harare and Bulawayo through this website only before  coming to VFS, without appointment VFS will not process your application.
- Applicant       must apply in Person.
- Payment mode Usd Cash Only for visa fees.
- Incomplete  applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in mind that failing to meet  any of the following requirements will also render the application incomplete:
- Please       be aware that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries       and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy.
- Please be aware that  applicants should be aware of the processing time for their chosen category of  visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are  minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- Please       be aware that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries       and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it       is not guaranteed that the embassy will grant your request
- Documents issued in a  Regional language must be officially translated to English.
- All       application forms filled by hand must be original in BLACK INK only.       Scanned copies or signatures of application forms will strictly not be       accepted.
- Frequent       Travelers – Applicant travelling more than once a year may apply for a       Multiple entries for up to 3 Years

## Photo Specifications
Click Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/54rKdHPXQfrGjZZPjbg9Xv/d9f77841ca2d2e1ba45abbd1ed698268/photo-spes.pdf)

## Processing Time
The timelines given by South African Embassy  in Zimbabwe for the processing of each application is minimum 7 working days from the day of submission at  Visa Application Centers.
However visa applicants should bear in  mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and  processing time may vary. Embassy Visa section may ask for additional documents  which may require more additional working days for processing.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

## ALL APPLICANT MUST APPLY IN PERSON
- Proof of financial – 3  months bank statement, certified by bank and not older than 7 days from the  time of application.
- Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear  in mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render  the application incomplete
- There must be 7 working days before the proposed flight itinerary;
- All supporting documents should be duly certified by the Police in South African Commissioner or Commissioner of Oaths;
- Invitation letter from host in South Africa must be signed;
- For minors, certified copy of birth certificate, certified copies of both biological parents’ Passports (main page) and 
an affidavit signed by both biological parents and certified by a Commissioner of Oaths (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4cYTp2dwSSDO57IbdAkDKr/ea55a168a2254e6d87db762c09c3f13c/parentalconsentaffidavit.pdf) will be required. Additional documents (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/southafrica/zimbabwe/english/pdf/childtravel.pdf)
 will be required in case either of the natural parents is unable to sign.
- No fixed flight  arrangements should be made until the visa has been attained.
- Payment mode Cash Only
- Please be aware that all  decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa  are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it is not guaranteed that the  embassy will grant your request.
- Please note applicants  should be aware of the Processing Time for their chosen category of visa and  apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are minimum as  stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- All application forms filled  by hand must be original in BLACK INK only. Scanned copies or signatures of  application forms will strictly not be accepted.
- Only applicants with a  Zimbabwe residential status can apply for a visa.
- Documents issued in a  foreign language must be officially translated to English by a sworn  translator.
- To request an appointment  for permanent residency, please send an email to:
info.sazim@vfshelpline.com (mailto:info.sazim@vfshelpline.com)

## WHO  QUALIFIES FOR THIS PERMIT?
All foreigners who wish to permanently reside  in South Africa who qualifies by virtue of complying with all necessary  requirements.

## APPLICATION  FOR DETERMINATION /CONFIRMATION OF PERMANENT RESIDENCE STATUS IN SOUTH AFRICA.
Please go to the Documents Required link  above to ascertain the requirements.

- Payment Options - Cash & Card only.

visa_types.documents_required:
- Passport,       (valid for at least 30 days after the intended stay/departure date from       South Africa; at least Two unused/blank visa pages)
- Fully completed visa application Form – 8 (DHA-1738) and DHA-84 must be completed in black ink and block letters.Only handwritten application forms are accepted in Black INK only.
- Certified birth certificate copy of the applicant
- Contract of Employment for the Host parent/Spouse
- Proof of funds (Last 03 month’s bank statement signed and stamped by the bank with a balance of South African Currency R 3000 or equivalent.Last date on the statement/Bank stamp date should not be older than 7 days when submitting.
- Invitation       letter from the host in South Africa. The letter must include       physical/street address, as well as contact details of the host with name,       designation and signature; and attested by commissioner of OATHS or south       African police(south Africa)
- Copy       of the host’s ID or copy of residential status, (permanent residence       permit or work permit and passport bio-data pages and Host ID copy       attested by commissioner of OATHS or South African Police (South Africa)
- Residence       proof of host form south Africa(attested)
- Vaccination certificate, (yellow fever) if applicant will travel through or stay in yellow fever belt. For the latest Yellow Fever Policy please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4LLo2aMcYoC6e6ap7XKTFD/0e3c4baea7e37e157c690b512d5e58a4/revised-yellow.pdf)
- Original       covering letter signed by the applicant.
- Marriage       certificate, where applicable (For verification purpose applicant needs to       carry the Original Marriage Certificate at the time of submission at VFS       Centre)
- A medical report completed on doctor’s letter head, Medical Report issued by registered medical practioner on letterhead with regard to applicant's general state of health. Report not older than 3 months at time of submission.All Medical and radiological reports must be supported with separate letter from doctor confirming that the reports have been issued based on personal examination by said doctor.
- Police clearance certificates in respect of applicants 18 years and older, in respect of all countries where person resided one year or longer since having attained the age of 18 Proof of Accommodation, eg undertaking from Host, copies of RSA Identification Document, copy of residence permit in RSA, copy of passport, utility bill (certified copies)
- Original       covering letter signed by the applicant stating the name, passport number       of applicant(s) and duration of visit. Letter must have the physical       address, as well as contact details of authorized signature’s name,       designation and signature (should clearly mention who will bear the       expenses of the trip)
Notes:
- Minor child applying for dependent visa where in inviter parent is in South Africa then they have to visit either Department of Home Affairs office or South African Police station in South Africa to get the Parental Consent Affidavit form attested. Once attestation is done they have to send clear scan attested copy to the parent available in Zimbabwe. During the time of submission at VFS that copy should be attached as part of the minor child application (mandatory). Once submission is done the other parent available in India does not need to visit Consulate to carry out the procedure.
- Minors who apply for a South African visa at VFS service-point shall be required to submit the attested copy of Parental Consent Affidavit less than 4 months old as part of the application.
- If minor child accompanied by someone other than a parent and both parent present in Zimbabwe then for Parental Consent Affidavit.
- If unabridged birth certificate is in local language than English translation affidavit must be produce along with local language unabridged birth certificate.

## Overview

- There must be 7 working days before the proposed  flight itinerary;
- All supporting documents should be duly  certified by the Police in South African  Commissioner or Commissioner of Oaths;
- Invitation letter from host in South Africa must be signed;
- No fixed flight  arrangements should be made until the visa has been attained.
- For minors, certified copy of birth certificate, certified  copies of both biological parents’ Passports (main page) and an affidavit signed by both biological parents and certified by  a Commissioner of Oaths (http://www.vfsglobal.com/southafrica/zimbabwe/pdf/parentalconsentaffidavit.pdf) will be  required. Additional documents (http://www.vfsglobal.com/southafrica/zimbabwe/pdf/childtravel.pdf) will be required in case either of the natural parents is unable  to sign.
- Payment       mode Cash Only
- Please       be aware that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries       and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it       is not guaranteed that the embassy will grant your request.
- Please       note applicants should be aware of the Processing Time for their       chosen category of visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all       processing times are minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- All       application forms filled by hand must be original in BLACK INK only.       Scanned copies or signatures of application forms will strictly not be       accepted.

## Visa Fees
- Visa Fee :- USD 24.00 (All Visa Fees are NON REFUNDABLE and they are only payable in USD)

visa_types.documents_required:
- Application       for DHA-1738 completed in full with BLACK ink and signed by applicant
- A       valid passport which expires in no less than 30 days after expiry of the       intended date of departure (at least Two unused/blank visa pages)
- Two       passport size coloured photographs (35X45mm)
- Original       covering letter signed by the applicant (should clearly mention the       purpose of visit)
- Proof       of sufficient financial means in the form of recently bank certified statements       for the last three months.
- Vaccination certificate, (yellow fever) if applicant will travel through or stay in yellow fever belt. For the latest Yellow Fever Policy please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4LLo2aMcYoC6e6ap7XKTFD/0e3c4baea7e37e157c690b512d5e58a4/revised-yellow.pdf)
- A medical report completed on doctor’s letter head, Medical Report issued by registered medical practioner on letterhead with regard to applicant's general state of health. Report not older than 6 months at time of submission.
- Marriage       certificate or in the case of a foreign spousal relationship, proof of       official recognition thereof issued by the authorities of the country       concerned if available
- The       affidavit where a spousal relationship to a South African citizen or       resident is applicable, as well as documentation proving cohabitation and       the extent to which the related financial responsibilities are shared by       the parties and setting out the particulars of children in the spousal       relationship
- Divorce       decree, where applicable
- Court       order granting full or specific parental responsibilities and rights,       where applicable
- Death       certificate, in respect of late spouse, where applicable
- Written       consent from both parents and court order granting full parental       responsibilities and rights parent, where applicable
- Proof       of legal adoption where applicable
- Legal       separation order, where applicable
- Police       clearance certificates in respect of applicants 18 years and older, in       respect of all countries where person resided one year or longer since       having attained the age of 18.
- A       written undertaking by the employer accepting responsibility for the costs       related to the deportation of the applicant and his or her dependent       family members, should it become necessary
- A       written undertaking by the employer to ensure that the passport of his or her       employee is valid at all times for the duration of his or her employment
- The       foreigner's contract of employment with the company abroad valid for a       period of not less than six months
- Letter       from the company abroad confirming that the applicant shall be transferred       to a branch, subsidiary or an affiliate of that company in South Africa
- A       letter from the branch, subsidiary or an affiliate in South Africa       confirming the transfer of the foreigner and specifying the occupation and       capacity in which the foreigner shall be employed
- An       undertaking from the employer that -
- He       foreigner shall only be employed in the specific position for which the       visa has been issued
- The       foreigner will, at all times, comply with the provisions of the Act and       conditions of his or her visa and undertakes to immediately notify the       Director-General if the employee refuses to comply with the provisions of       the Act or conditions of the visa;
- A       plan is developed for the transfer of skills to a South African citizen or       permanent
- An       undertaking from the branch, subsidiary or an affiliate in South Africa to       reimburse the Department any costs incurred in relation to the deportation       of the holder of an intra-company transfer work visa and any of his or her       family members

- Applicant needs to book  the appointment for Harare and Bulawayo through this website only before coming  to VFS, without appointment VFS will not process your application.

- Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in  mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render  the application incomplete:
- All the  supporting documents should be duly certified by the Police in South African  Commissioner or commissioner of Oaths.
- Please be aware       that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and       duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy.
- Please be aware that  applicants should be aware of the processing time for their chosen category of  visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are  minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- Please be aware       that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and       duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it is       not guaranteed that the embassy will grant your request
- Documents issued in a  Regional language must be officially translated to English.
- All application       forms filled by hand must be original in BLACK INK only. Scanned copies or       signatures of application forms will strictly not be accepted.

- There is an additional service charge of $75.00 (inclusive of TAX) per application in addition to the visa fees for all applicants. This fee must be paid only at the Visa Application Centre
- The Special Delivery facility for the return of Passports is available at a fee of $10 (Inclusive of TAX) per application. This is an optional service and can be paid at the Visa Application Centre.
- The SMS facility for updates on your application is available at a fee of $2 (Inclusive of TAX) per application. This is an optional service and can be paid at the Visa Application Centre.
- Payment Options – USD Cash only for visa fees.

## Documents Required
- Passport,       (valid for at least 30 days after the intended stay/departure date from       South Africa; at least Two unused/blank visa pages)
- Two       recent (not older than 30 days/1 month) passport size, ( 35mm x 45mm of       the picture) photographs
- Fully completed visa application form-11 (DHA-84) – must be completed in black ink and block letters.  Only handwritten application forms are accepted in Black INK only.
- Application       form-11 (DHA-84) must be signed by the applicant and signature should       match the signature on the passport.
- Vaccination certificate, (yellow fever) if applicant will travel through or stay in yellow fever belt. For the latest Yellow Fever Policy please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2153ZdhqdPh90PtjNSfHAb/048510c493a213044f96b7c37e0d61a6/visitor-visa-health-africa.pdf)
- Proof       of financial – 3 months bank statement, certified by bank and not older       than 7 days from the time of application. Incomplete applications will not       be accepted.
- There       must be 7 working days before the proposed flight itinerary;
- No       fixed flight arrangements should be made until the visa has been       attained.
- All       supporting documents should be duly  certified by the Police in South       African Commissioner or Commissioner of Oaths;
- Invitation       letter from host in South Africa must be signed;
- Marriage       certificate, where applicable (For verification purpose applicant needs to       carry the Original Marriage Certificate at the time of submission at VFS       center)
- Copies       of passport bio-data pages
- Original       covering letter signed by the applicant stating the name, passport number       of applicant(s) and duration of visit. Letter must have the physical       address, as well as contact details of authorized signature’s name,       designation and signature (should clearly mention who will bear the       expenses of the trip)
- For       Minor Dependent Children
- Documents such as Parental Consent Affidavit form for which the link is below, passport copy of parents as well as child + Unabridged Birth Certificate of child must be carried for Parental Consent Affidavit procedure. In case of death of either parents death certificate has to be produced. In case of death of both parents the procedure has to be carried out by legal guardian at Mumbai Consulate and death certificates mandatory of both parents to be produced. In case of divorce the legal divorce documents has to be produced.
- Affidavit less than 3 months old as part of the application. Originals should be kept for their records while travelling Minors who apply for a South African visa at VFS service-point shall be required to submit the attested copy of Parental Consent to South Africa.
- If unabridged birth certificate is in local language than English translation affidavit must be produce along with local language unabridged birth certificate.
Some Important Points
- Applicant needs to book the appointment for Harare and Bulawayo through this website only before coming to VFS, without appointment VFS will not process your application.
- Applicant must apply in Person.
- Payment mode USD Cash Only for visa fees.
- Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render the application incomplete:
- Please be aware that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy.
- Please be aware that applicants should be aware of the processing time for their chosen category of visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- Please be aware that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it is not guaranteed that the embassy will grant your request
- Documents issued in a Regional language must be officially translated to English.
- All application forms filled by hand must be original in BLACK INK only. Scanned copies or signatures of application forms will strictly not be accepted.
- Frequent Travelers – Applicant travelling more than once a year may apply for a Multiple entries for up to 3 Years

## Photo Specifications

Click Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/54rKdHPXQfrGjZZPjbg9Xv/d9f77841ca2d2e1ba45abbd1ed698268/photo-spes.pdf)

## Processing Time
The timelines given by South African Embassy  in Zimbabwe for the processing of each application is minimum 7 working days  from the day of submission at Visa Application Centers.
However visa applicants should bear in  mind that each application is subject to an individual assessment and  processing time may vary. Embassy Visa section may ask for additional documents  which may require more additional working days for processing.
